default.name: stdio only
default.context: Standard for Claude Code local use. Server launches per-session. Simplest to develop and test.
default.when: Personal/local tool, single user, no remote access needed

default.name: stdio + HTTP/SSE
default.context: Supports both local Claude Code use and remote hosting. More complex but covers all deployment scenarios.
default.when: Need remote access, multiple clients, or homelab deployment

default.name: HTTP/SSE only
default.context: Remote-only server. Cannot be used as a local stdio server. Requires always-on hosting.
default.when: Centralized server for multiple users or always-on service

default.name: SQLite (better-sqlite3)
default.context: Single-file embedded database. Zero-config, portable, fast reads. Best for local-first MCP servers with structured data.
default.when: Most MCP servers with persistent state

default.name: JSON files
default.context: Maximum simplicity and portability. Easy to inspect and edit manually. No query capability beyond full scan.
default.when: Simple key-value data, <100 records, or configuration-only persistence

default.name: PostgreSQL (Supabase/Neon)
default.context: Full SQL, concurrent access, managed hosting. Adds operational complexity and external dependency.
default.when: Multi-user servers, complex queries, or cloud-hosted deployment

typescript.name: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (TypeScript)
typescript.context: Official MCP SDK. Most mature, best documented, aligns with Anthropic ecosystem. First to receive protocol updates.

python.name: mcp (Python)
python.context: Official Python MCP SDK. Good for data-heavy servers or Python-native integrations.

default.name: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (TypeScript)
default.context: TypeScript SDK is the most mature and best documented. Recommended default.

default.name: Manual trigger tool only
default.context: User invokes an update tool when they want fresh data. Simplest, no background process needed.

default.name: External cron + manual trigger
default.context: OS cron or container cron runs a CLI command on a schedule. Server doesn't need to be long-running for stdio.

default.name: In-process scheduling (node-cron) + manual trigger
default.context: Built-in scheduler for long-running HTTP/SSE servers. Adds complexity but is self-contained.
default.when: Always-on HTTP/SSE servers needing periodic background tasks

default.name: No internal LLM calls
default.context: Server provides data and lets the client's LLM do the analysis. Simpler, no API costs.
default.when: Data retrieval tools, formatters, simple lookups

default.name: Anthropic API (Haiku for high-volume, Sonnet for analysis)
default.context: Use Haiku for classification/extraction (~$0.25/M input tokens), Sonnet for complex analysis (~$3/M input tokens). Mix models by task.
default.when: Servers that need to analyze, compare, or generate recommendations

default.name: Anthropic API (Opus for deep analysis)
default.context: Most capable model (~$15/M input tokens). Reserve for tasks where quality justifies cost.
default.when: Complex advisory, architectural analysis, nuanced recommendations

default.name: Local only (stdio via Claude Code)
default.context: No hosting needed. Server runs locally when Claude Code starts a session.

default.name: Docker on homelab/VPS
default.context: Self-hosted, always-on, full control. Docker Compose for easy management.
default.when: Homelab, LXC, or VPS with existing infrastructure

default.name: Railway or Render
default.context: Managed cloud hosting. Simple deployment, $5-20/month. No infrastructure management.

typescript.name: Zod
typescript.context: TypeScript-first schema validation. Single source of truth for types and runtime validation. Integrates with MCP SDK.

default.name: Zod (TypeScript) or Pydantic (Python)
default.context: Language-native schema validation. Provides both type safety and runtime validation.
